project.name: LinkPulse
project.description: Link-in-bio analytics platform. Users create a public page with a list of links and get detailed click analytics (geo, referrer, time series).
project.backendStackPreference: FastAPI + PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy + ARQ (async Redis queue)
project.notes: Single-tenant by user. Each user has one public link page at /{username}. Click tracking endpoint is public (no auth) and high-volume. API keys allow programmatic link management for integrations.

pages.name: Register
pages.purpose: Create a new account and choose a username for the public link page

pages.name: Dashboard
pages.purpose: Overview: KPI cards (total clicks today / this week / all time), sparkline chart of clicks last 14 days, top 5 links by clicks
emptyStates: no clicks yet — share your page

pages.name: LinkEditor
pages.purpose: Manage the list of links: create, reorder (drag & drop), toggle active, edit, delete. Preview panel shows live public page appearance.
emptyStates: no links yet — add your first link

pages.name: Analytics
pages.purpose: Detailed analytics: click volume chart (daily/weekly/monthly toggle), geographic breakdown (country bar chart), top referrers table, top links table, date range picker

pages.name: ApiKeys
pages.purpose: Manage API keys for programmatic access: list keys (prefix, scopes, last used), create new key (shown once after creation), revoke

pages.name: ProfileSettings
pages.purpose: Edit profile: display name, bio (max 160 chars), avatar upload, public page theme (color preset selector), email notification preferences

forms.name: RegisterForm
forms.successBehavior: Redirect to /dashboard with email verification prompt

forms.name: LinkForm
forms.successBehavior: Link appears/updates in list, preview panel refreshes

forms.name: ApiKeyCreateForm
forms.successBehavior: Show full key once (CreatedKeyReveal component), then show in masked list

authFlows.notes: JWT access token (15 min) + refresh token (7 days) in HttpOnly cookie
Sends verification email via ARQ task. Dashboard accessible before verification but with persistent banner.
Silent refresh via HttpOnly cookie before access token expires
GET /auth/verify-email?token=... link in email. Token expires in 24 hours.

externalTriggers.name: PublicClickTracking
externalTriggers.triggerSource: Public page visitor clicks a link → POST /p/:username/:linkId/click
externalTriggers.expectedOutcome: ClickEvent row written async via ARQ task (non-blocking); link clickCount incremented

externalTriggers.name: ResendEmailDelivery
externalTriggers.triggerSource: Register → email verification; milestone → notification email; cron → weekly digest

externalTriggers.name: WeeklyDigestCron
externalTriggers.triggerSource: Every Monday 09:00 UTC — ARQ scheduled job
externalTriggers.expectedOutcome: Weekly click summary email sent to all users with notifyWeeklyDigest = true

assumptions: Each user has exactly one public link page at /{username} — no multi-page support in v1
Click tracking is fire-and-forget: POST /p/:username/:linkId/click returns 204 immediately; ClickEvent is written by an ARQ task (eventual consistency acceptable)
Analytics aggregation (AnalyticsSummary table) is pre-computed hourly by an ARQ cron job — raw ClickEvent table is not queried directly by the analytics API
Redis cache layer sits in front of analytics queries — cache TTL 5 minutes; invalidated on new click batch
API keys are hashed with SHA-256 before storage — only the prefix (first 8 chars) is stored in plaintext for display
Avatar and link thumbnails are stored in a public S3 bucket — no signed URLs, direct CDN access
No compliance requirements (no PHI, no PCI-DSS) — standard security baseline applies
Rate limiting on click tracking endpoint: 200 req/min per IP via Redis sliding window — above threshold returns 429
Idempotency key required on POST /links to prevent double-creation from network retry
Weekly digest is dispatched via ARQ scheduled job with deterministic job_id — double-enqueue is prevented
